name: Snow Report 2026-03-09 05:34:02
report:
Good morning, Beast family,

It was another killer day in the books yesterday, filled with soft skiing and riding under plenty of sunshine. We lucked out with great conditions this weekend— about as good of a kickoff to the spring action you can ask for, and we are still leading the East with the most open terrain.

Sunny skies are back today with temperatures climbing to the low-to-mid 40s. However, it’s going to be quite breezy out there with southwest winds gusting up to 50 mph at times.

Yesterday’s snow conditions were again soft and easy to carve making for tons of fun. With all that sun, areas were moving a little slower than previous days and natural terrain has traces of thin cover, although we still have plenty of wall to wall runs and good depths to keep ripping through spring. Cruise Control was a blast to arc up as always and Bunny Buster offered wide turns spanning the slope that led nicely into steeper terrain on Mouse Trap. Skyehawk had those nice natural moguls to bump up so don’t forget about that black diamond run, it’s really fun. Groomed trails are moving faster than natural ones and are the go to for deep turns. Our terrain should remain in similar shape today with tons of sun and comfortable temps with great shredding on the way. 

Our grooming team is after trails that have seen snowmaking, staying off natural terrain to keep the depths on those trails as strong as possible. Some cruisers that will be resurfaced overnight include Grizzly, Skyelark, and Full House with steeper options being Lower Wildfire and East Fall. Groomed runs will again be easy to turn on and among the best choices. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. Temps have risen out of range, and our snowmaking team has brought the system offline. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us over the next couple of weeks, but we're not done making snow yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

The Stash will have a delayed opening this morning so that we can let it soften up and rework it after the Slash & Berm Banked Slalom this weekend— with an estimated 11 am opening over there. The Lil' Stash will also be closed after yesterday’s Slash & Berm Grom Slalom to get a refresh.  

The 2026 Spring Pass is on sale for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade at any time for just $199. March 12 is the last day to score the best price at $379. This winter is far from finished!

See you out there!

 – Josh

 

Operating Day: 118

Lifts      

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.     

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.      

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!      

The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22. 

Uphill Travel      

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.   

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. name: Snow Report 2026-03-08 05:50:12
report:
Good morning, Killington,

We had an incredible day of skiing & riding at The Beast yesterday. The sun came out for most of the day making for soft and carve friendly snow. It’s officially Spring skiing out there and we are absolutely psyched to be shredding the most terrain in the East. 

Cloudy skies will start the day before gradually clearing by late morning, with temperatures falling to around the mid-40s by the afternoon. Expect a windy day on the mountain, with west winds of 29 to 38 mph and strong gusts keeping things feeling a little brisk.

The slopes were riding extremely well yesterday with light and soft surfaces that were easy to dig into and moving quite fast in some areas. It feels like Spring has arrived, especially on Outer Limits with natural bumps on the right, a smooth line for carving down the middle, and procured moguls on the lower left, it really has everything. Skyelark also had snow that was easy and fun to shred along with the offerings in Needle’s Eye. Our groomed surfaces are looking great, and there are traces of thin cover on our natural terrain, but we still have strong coverage across the mountain and surfaces will stay soft today so you really can’t go wrong. 

Our grooming team made corduroy across the mountain and left Downdraft and Double Dipper in the Canyon on natural terrain. Fun cruisers like Bear Claw and Rime will be smooth for you to carve up in the morning. A faster and steeper run that we also resurfaced is Cascade and if that’s the kind of run your after, you can’t go wrong with Superstar. Groomed runs are extra buttery to sink the edges into and any of the pickings should be a blast. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. Temps have risen out of range, and our snowmaking team has brought the system offline. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us over the next couple of weeks, but we're not done making snow yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off.

Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

The Stash is currently closed after the Slash & Berm Banked Slalom yesterday. Congratulations to all those that shredded the course! The Stash will remain closed as the park undergoes a refresh and returns to its classic form. The Lil' Stash will be closed today, March 8th, for the Slash & Berm Grom Slalom. 

The 2026 Spring Pass is on sale for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade at any time for just $199. March 12 is the last day to score the best price at $379. This winter is far from finished!

To celebrate International Women’s Day, we’ll be hosting a party lap led by some of the incredible women on the Killington Team. If any of you lady shredders want to join, meet at the top of Snowdon at 10:00 a.m. today, March 8th.  

It's unreal at The Beast! 

 – Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-07 16:00:11
report:
What’s up, Killington,

We had an incredible day of skiing & riding at The Beast. The sun came out for most of the day making for soft and carve friendly snow. It’s officially Spring skiing out there and we are absolutely psyched to be shredding the most terrain in the East. 

Cloudy skies will start the day before gradually clearing by late morning, with temperatures falling to around the mid-40s by the afternoon. Expect a windy day on the mountain, with strong west winds of 29 to 38 mph and gusts keeping things feeling a little brisk.

The slopes were riding extremely well today with light and soft surfaces that were easy to dig into and moving quite fast in some areas. It feels like Spring has arrived, especially on Outer Limits with natural bumps on the right, a smooth line for carving down the middle, and procured moguls on the lower left, it really has everything. Skyelark also had snow that was easy and fun to shred along with the offerings in Needle’s Eye. Surfaces will stay soft and perfect tomorrow so you really can’t go wrong. 

Our grooming team plans to lay corduroy down across the mountain and will leave Downdraft and Double Dipper in the Canyon on natural terrain. Fun cruisers like Bear Claw and Rime will be smooth by the time you get to them. A faster and steeper run that we also resurfaced is Cascade and if that’s the kind of run your after, you can’t go wrong with Superstar. Groomed runs are extra buttery to sink the edges into and any of the pickings should be a blast. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. Temps have risen out of range, and our snowmaking team has brought the system offline. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us over the next couple of weeks, but we're not done making snow yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

The Stash is currently closed after the Slash & Berm Banked Slalom today. It was a hype and energetic event, congratulations to all those that shredded the course! The Stash will remain closed as the park undergoes construction and returns to its classic form. The Lil' Stash will be closed tomorrow, March 8th, for the Slash & Berm Grom Slalom. 

The 2026 Spring Pass is on sale for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade at any time for just $199. March 12 is the last day to score the best price at $379. This winter is far from finished!

To celebrate International Women’s Day, we’ll be hosting a party lap led by some of the incredible women on the Killington Team. If any of you lady shredders want to join, meet at the top of Snowdon at 10:00 a.m. tomorrow, March 8th.  

It's unreal at The Beast! 

 – Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-06 05:50:35
report:
Good morning, Beast family,

It was another clear and comfortable day on the mountain with great snow to shred under the sunshine on Thursday. An inch of snow moved in last night along with a brief mix of sleet and freezing rain. There's another chance of snow early today, then it'll be mostly cloudy with highs around 29°F at the base and 33°F at the top, and a breezy southeast wind should keep things feeling like midwinter. Stay dialed in on the happenings across the mountain right here as we have another fun weekend approaching. 

Skiing & riding was a joy yesterday with perfect packed powder to choose from on trails like Skyelark and softer spring-like snow and bumps on Outer Limits. We're expecting more great conditions today thanks to the touch of fresh snow overnight. Due to the interesting weather last night, conditions may vary across the mountain from packed powder to firm snow. Higher elevations received more snow overnight and should resemble mid winter conditions, while lower elevations have traces of crust, especially on natural terrain. Ski Patrol will be monitoring the hill this morning, so be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

Our team of groomers tackled just under 80 trails last night, grooming some runs twice to mitigate crusting and keeping our cruisers riding smooth and soft. They hit Superstar last night for the first time since our latest snowmaking run began, and will continue to move those massive whales of snowmaking around. Bear Mountain was providing great surfaces yesterday and should be on your radar today along with other groomed runs from Easy Street to Pipe Dream. They also got after Outer Limits and made moguls for those looking to get some practice in ahead of the Bear Mogul Challenge, which is under a month away. 

Key lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m. The South Ridge and Canyon Quads will be spinning at 10:00 a.m. 

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. In the past week, the whales on Superstar have piled up tremendously, with some now standing taller than the new Superstar Six towers! Temps have risen out of range and our snowmaking team has brought the system offline. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us over the next couple of weeks, but we're not done making snow yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

The Stash is currently closed for this year's Slash & Berm Banked Slalom today and Saturday, March 6th and 7th. The Lil' Stash will close this Sunday for the Slash & Berm Grom Slalom on March 8th. Limited spots are still open for Saturday's open competition, but if you want to get as close to the action as you can, consider signing up to volunteer as a course marshal, where each completed shift will earn you a FREE Killington lift ticket voucher, as well as lunch for the day.

The 2026 Spring Pass is on sale for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade at any time for just $199. March 12 is the last day to score the best price at $379. This winter is far from finished!

We have another banger set lined up on the K-1 patio this Saturday featuring DJ Chaston from 2:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. Get ready to bring your energy outside.  

To celebrate International Women’s Day, we’ll be hosting a party lap led by some of the incredible women on the Killington Team. If any of you lady shredders want to join, meet at the top of Snowdon near the trial sign at 10:00 a.m. on Sunday, March 8th.  

There's a lot to look forward to this weekend, but for now it's time to make the most of your Friday!

 – Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-05 05:49:51
report:
Good morning, Beast fans,

The perfect weather to be sliding down the hill and 4 inches of snow were just what we needed yesterday. It was all time out there, just beautiful weather and unbelievable skiing & riding. 

Today brings a mix of sun and clouds, with inverted highs of 37 at the peak and 35 at the base. Temperatures will be sliding down to around 25 degrees by late afternoon. A light northwest breeze in the morning will shift to the east as the day rolls on, keeping things crisp while you stack those afternoon laps.

The slopes were covered in packed powder and soft snow under the sun as Wednesday progressed. Cascade had really fun powdery surfaces to turn on and Snowdon was a blast to lap with spring-like conditions in the afternoon. Gateway was a nice cruiser to enjoy under the sunshine as well. Head over to sunny trails like Outer Limits and those on the South Ridge for the best runs today. Temps are expected to stay around or below freezing and ungroomed trails in the shade will not see much relief from the warmer temps, and may be on the firm side. Trails in the sun will likely be softer. 

Our grooming team included Downdraft in the long list of runs that are resurfaced this morning. The groomers were smooth early on and turned into amazing packed powder as the snow loosened up yesterday. As that new snow continues to get worked we can expect to rip machine groomed packed powder that’s very carve friendly. 

Key lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m. The South Ridge Quad will be spinning at 10:00 a.m. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. In the past week, the whales on Superstar have piled up tremendously, with some now standing taller than the new Superstar Six towers! Since yesterday morning, temps have risen out of range and our snowmaking team has brought the system offline. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us over the next couple of weeks, but we're not done making snow yet. 

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

The Stash is currently closed so our crew can build the course for this year's Slash & Berm Banked Slalom on March 6th-7th. The Lil' Stash will close this weekend for the Slash & Berm Grom Slalom on March 8th. Limited spots are still open for Saturday's open competition, but if you want to get as close to the action as you can, consider signing up to volunteer as a course marshal, where each completed shift will earn you a FREE Killington lift ticket voucher, as well as lunch for the day.

With conditions looking this good, now’s a great time to secure your Spring Pass here for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13 through the end of the season. Prices will increase after March 12th so don’t wait. 

It’s really good out there, have at it! 

 – Evan

 

Operating Day: 114

Lifts      

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.     

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.      

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!      

The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22.
